title: Handoff Context v1
description: Structured context for cross-agent task handoffs in Canopy. Separates derived state (from ecosystem tools) from agent-authored intent and boundary. Producer: handing-off agent + ecosystem auto-population. Consumer: receiving agent + canopy + cap.

work_state.description: Derived from ecosystem tools (rhizome, cortina), not written by the agent. Represents what exists right now.

path.description: File path relative to project root.

symbols_changed.description: Symbol names changed in this file (from rhizome, optional).

modified_files.description: Files modified during this work unit. Derived from git diff or rhizome get_changed_files.

not_run.description: True if tests were never executed.

test_status.description: Test results from cortina outcome signals. Null if no test signal captured.

last_error.description: Most recent build error message, truncated.

build_status.description: Build status from cortina outcome signals. Null if no build signal captured.

intent.description: Agent-authored. What was being attempted and why.

goal.description: What the task is trying to achieve. Should match the canopy task description.

approach.description: How the agent was approaching the goal. Implementation strategy, patterns chosen, trade-offs made.

items.description: Council message ID referencing a Decision.

decisions_made.description: Pointers to council decisions that shaped the approach.

boundary.description: Agent-authored. Where work stopped and what the receiving agent should know.

blocked_on.description: What is preventing progress. Required when stop_reason is 'blocked'.

next_steps.description: Ordered list of what the receiving agent should do next.

warnings.description: Things the receiving agent should avoid. 'Don't touch X, it'll break Y.'

evidence.description: Existing canopy evidence references attached to this handoff.
